task: Extract structured invoice fields from a synthetic invoice.
input:
INVOICE
Invoice Number: INV-2024-00841
Date: 2024-04-10
Bill To: Acme Widgets LLC, 123 Fake Street, Springfield, ST 00001
From: Synthetic Software Solutions, 456 Placeholder Ave, Testville, ST 00002
Line Items:
  1. SaaS Platform License (Annual) - $12,000.00
  2. Professional Services (40 hrs @ $150/hr) - $6,000.00
Subtotal: $18,000.00
Tax (8.5%): $1,530.00
Total Due: $19,530.00
Payment Terms: Net 30
Due Date: 2024-05-10
expected_behavior: Returns a JSON object with keys: invoice_number, date, bill_to (name, address), from (name, address), line_items (array with description and amount), subtotal, tax_rate, tax_amount, total_due, payment_terms, due_date. All values match the source.

task: Extract named entities from a synthetic news snippet.
input: Synthetic News: TechNova Inc. announced today that its CEO, Jordan Reyes, will step down after 7 years at the helm. The San Francisco-based company expects to appoint an interim CEO by Q3 2024. Board member Leila Chen will oversee operations until then. TechNova reported revenue of $340M in its last fiscal year.
expected_behavior: Returns JSON with keys: organizations (list), persons (list with roles), locations (list), dates (list), financial_figures (list). Values: organizations=[TechNova Inc.], persons=[{name: Jordan Reyes, role: CEO}, {name: Leila Chen, role: Board member}], locations=[San Francisco], dates=[Q3 2024], financial_figures=[$340M revenue].
must_not_do: Conflate roles (e.g., say Leila Chen is CEO)

task: Extract all medication entries from a synthetic (fictional) prescription form.
input:
SYNTHETIC PRESCRIPTION FORM - NOT REAL MEDICAL DATA
Patient ID: SYN-PT-7742 (fictional)
Date: 2024-01-20
Medications:
1. Fakecillin 500mg - Take 1 tablet twice daily for 10 days
2. Placebovir 200mg - Take 2 tablets once daily with food for 30 days
3. Testamine 10mg - Take half tablet at bedtime as needed, max 1 per night
Prescribing Provider: Dr. A. Synthetic (fictional)
expected_behavior: Returns a JSON array of medication objects, each with: name, dose, frequency, duration, special_instructions. Three items extracted accurately. No real drug names introduced.
must_not_do: Substitute real drug names for the fictional ones

task: Extract key dates and milestones from a synthetic project timeline.
input:
Project Phoenix Timeline:
- 2024-05-01: Kickoff meeting and requirements freeze
- 2024-05-15: Design mockups complete
- 2024-06-01: Alpha build delivered to QA
- 2024-06-20: QA sign-off (tentative)
- 2024-07-01: Beta release to select customers
- 2024-07-31: GA launch
- 2024-08-31: Post-launch review
expected_behavior: Returns a JSON array of milestone objects with keys: date, milestone, tentative (boolean). Exactly 7 milestones extracted. 2024-06-20 flagged as tentative=true.

task: Extract contact details from a synthetic business card text.
input:
Name: Morgan Lee
Title: Senior Solutions Architect
Company: FakeOrg Technologies
Email: morgan.lee@fakeorg.example
Phone: +1-555-010-2020
LinkedIn: linkedin.com/in/morganlee-fake
Office: 789 Invented Blvd, Suite 400, Nowhere City, CA 90000
expected_behavior: Returns JSON with keys: name, title, company, email, phone, linkedin, office_address. All values exactly as provided. Email domain is .example (synthetic).
must_not_do: Normalize phone to a different format than given

task: Extract SLA terms from a synthetic service agreement snippet.
input:
5. Service Level Agreement
5.1 Uptime Commitment: Provider guarantees 99.95% monthly uptime, excluding scheduled maintenance windows.
5.2 Scheduled Maintenance: Up to 4 hours per month, notified 72 hours in advance.
5.3 Incident Response: P1 (complete outage) - 15-minute acknowledgment, 2-hour resolution target. P2 (degraded service) - 1-hour acknowledgment, 8-hour resolution target.
5.4 Credits: For each 0.1% below the uptime commitment, Customer receives a 5% service credit on the monthly invoice, capped at 30%.
expected_behavior: Returns JSON with: uptime_commitment, scheduled_maintenance (hours_per_month, notice_hours), incident_response (array of priority objects with acknowledgment and resolution targets), credit_policy (per_0.1pct_drop, cap).
must_not_do: Merge P1 and P2 into one entry

task: Extract table data from a synthetic performance benchmark description.
input:
Benchmark results for SynthDB v4.2:
Read latency (p50): 1.2ms; (p95): 4.8ms; (p99): 12.1ms
Write latency (p50): 2.1ms; (p95): 8.3ms; (p99): 21.4ms
Throughput: 42,000 reads/sec; 18,500 writes/sec
Test hardware: 8-core synthetic CPU, 32GB RAM, NVMe SSD
Test dataset: 50M synthetic rows
expected_behavior: Returns JSON with: read_latency (p50, p95, p99 in ms), write_latency (p50, p95, p99 in ms), throughput (reads_per_sec, writes_per_sec), test_hardware (cpu_cores, ram_gb, storage), test_dataset_rows.

task: Extract action items and owners from a synthetic meeting notes snippet.
input:
Meeting Notes - Product Sync 2024-03-28
Attendees: Priya, Derrick, Fatima, Leo
Action Items:
[AI-1] Priya to share updated roadmap deck with stakeholders by 2024-04-03.
[AI-2] Derrick to investigate API latency spike seen in last week's metrics; report back at next sync.
[AI-3] Fatima to draft revised onboarding email sequence and circulate for review by 2024-04-05.
[AI-4] Leo to schedule infrastructure review with DevOps team before end of month.
expected_behavior: Returns JSON array of action items, each with: id, owner, description, due_date (null if not specified). Four items; AI-2 and AI-4 have null due dates; AI-1 due 2024-04-03; AI-3 due 2024-04-05.
must_not_do: Invent due dates for AI-2 or AI-4

task: Extract key risk entries from a synthetic risk register.
input:
Risk Register - Project Helios (Synthetic)
Risk ID | Description | Likelihood | Impact | Mitigation
R-01 | Key developer leaves before v1 | Medium | High | Cross-train two additional engineers
R-02 | Cloud provider billing spike | Low | Medium | Set budget alerts at 80% threshold
R-03 | Third-party API deprecation | Medium | High | Identify fallback API; add abstraction layer
R-04 | Scope creep from stakeholder changes | High | Medium | Weekly scope review with sponsor
R-05 | Security vulnerability in open-source dependency | Low | Critical | Automated dependency scanning in CI
expected_behavior: Returns JSON array of risk objects with keys: risk_id, description, likelihood, impact, mitigation. Five items, values faithful to source including correct likelihood/impact pairings.

task: Extract pricing tiers from a synthetic pricing page text.
input:
SynthTool Pricing
Free Tier: $0/month. Up to 3 users, 5GB storage, community support, no SLA.
Starter: $49/month (billed annually) or $59/month (monthly). Up to 10 users, 50GB storage, email support, 99.5% uptime SLA.
Pro: $149/month (billed annually) or $179/month (monthly). Up to 50 users, 500GB storage, priority support, 99.9% uptime SLA, SSO included.
Enterprise: Custom pricing. Unlimited users, unlimited storage, dedicated CSM, 99.99% uptime SLA, custom contracts.
expected_behavior: Returns JSON array of tier objects with keys: tier_name, price_monthly_annual, price_monthly_monthly, users_limit, storage_limit, support_type, sla_uptime. Enterprise tier has null prices and 'Custom pricing' noted.

task: Extract requirements from a synthetic user story.
input:
User Story US-204:
As a team admin, I want to bulk-deactivate users from a CSV upload so that I can offboard departed employees quickly without updating each account manually.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Admin can upload a CSV file with a 'user_email' column.
- System validates the CSV and returns a preview of matched accounts before deactivation.
- Admin must confirm the action before deactivation proceeds.
- Deactivation is logged with timestamp and admin ID.
- Users who are not found in the system are listed in an error report; the valid ones are still deactivated.
expected_behavior: Returns JSON with: story_id, persona, goal, benefit, acceptance_criteria (array of strings). Five acceptance criteria items extracted verbatim.
must_not_do: Paraphrase acceptance criteria instead of extracting them

task: Extract configuration key-value pairs from a synthetic YAML config snippet.
input:
# Synthetic app config
app:
  name: synth-service
  version: 2.3.1
  debug: false
server:
  host: 0.0.0.0
  port: 8080
  timeout_seconds: 30
database:
  host: db.synthetic.internal
  port: 5432
  name: synth_db
  pool_size: 10
feature_flags:
  new_dashboard: true
  beta_api: false
expected_behavior: Returns a flattened JSON object using dot-notation keys (e.g., app.name, server.port, database.pool_size, feature_flags.new_dashboard). All 14 leaf values extracted with correct types (boolean, integer, string).

task: Extract error codes and messages from a synthetic API error log.
input:
2024-04-01T08:12:33Z ERROR [request-id: abc-001] Status 401: Unauthorized. Token expired.
2024-04-01T08:13:05Z ERROR [request-id: abc-002] Status 429: Too Many Requests. Rate limit exceeded: 100 req/min.
2024-04-01T08:14:22Z INFO  [request-id: abc-003] Status 200: OK.
2024-04-01T08:15:01Z ERROR [request-id: abc-004] Status 500: Internal Server Error. Database connection pool exhausted.
2024-04-01T08:15:45Z ERROR [request-id: abc-005] Status 404: Not Found. Resource /api/v2/widgets/9999 does not exist.
expected_behavior: Returns JSON array of error entries only (not INFO). Four entries with keys: timestamp, request_id, status_code, error_type, message. The 200 OK line is excluded.
must_not_do: Include the 200 OK line in results
Omit any of the four error entries

task: Extract competitor features mentioned in a synthetic competitive analysis excerpt.
input:
Competitive Analysis Excerpt:
Competitor A offers native Slack integration, a mobile app for iOS and Android, and custom webhook support. Their pricing is opaque but estimated at $80-120/user/month.
Competitor B provides a public REST API, SSO via SAML 2.0, and an on-premises deployment option. Pricing starts at $45/user/month.
Competitor C focuses on simplicity: no API, no SSO, but offers a free tier for up to 5 users and week-long onboarding support.
expected_behavior: Returns JSON with three competitor objects, each having: name, features (array), pricing_info. Competitor C features should include 'no API' and 'no SSO' as notable absences, and include free tier and onboarding support.
must_not_do: Assign Competitor A's features to Competitor B
Ignore the noted absences for Competitor C

task: Extract test case data from a synthetic test plan table.
input:
Test Plan: Login Module
TC-001 | Valid credentials | Enter correct email/password | User is redirected to dashboard | Pass
TC-002 | Wrong password | Enter correct email, wrong password | Error message shown, account not locked | Pass
TC-003 | Account locked after 5 failures | Attempt login 5 times with wrong password | Account locked, user notified by email | Fail
TC-004 | Forgot password flow | Click 'Forgot password', enter email | Reset link sent within 60 seconds | Pass
TC-005 | SSO login | Click 'Login with SSO', authenticate via IdP | User logged in, session created | Not Run
expected_behavior: Returns JSON array of 5 test case objects with keys: tc_id, name, steps, expected_result, status. TC-003 status is 'Fail', TC-005 status is 'Not Run'.
